# ==========================================================================
# requirements.txt  -  GENERATED LOCKFILE (do not hand-edit)
# ==========================================================================
# This file should be produced from `templates/requirements.in` by
# `pip-compile` (pip-tools) or `uv pip compile`. Do NOT edit by hand;
# re-run the compile step and commit the result.
#
# Generate with one of:
#     pip-compile requirements.in --output-file requirements.txt
#     uv pip compile requirements.in -o requirements.txt
#
# Install into a fresh venv with one of:
#     pip-sync requirements.txt
#     uv pip sync requirements.txt
#
# Every line below (once generated) will be an EXACT pin with a hash, e.g.:
#     numpy==1.26.4 \
#         --hash=sha256:abcd...
#
# That hash-checking mode is what buys you reproducibility:
#   - If PyPI is compromised or a release is yanked, the install fails
#     closed rather than installing a different artifact.
#   - Reviewers get byte-identical wheels to the ones used at /execute.
#
# Record the sha256 of this file (after it is regenerated) in
# execution-log.yaml under environment_capture.environment_file_digest.
# ==========================================================================
#
# This placeholder is intentionally empty. Run the compile command above
# before `/execute` begins; commit the generated result.
